toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Run dotnet test with crash dump collection enabled

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Run dotnet test with crash dump collection enabled

data.reasoningText:
The user wants to run tests with `dotnet test` and collect a crash dump if any test crashes the host process. 

For .NET, crash dump collection can be configured via environment variables. The standard approach is to use the `DOTNET_DbgEnableMiniDump` environment variable (or `COMPlus_DbgEnableMiniDump` for older runtimes) to enable crash dump collection.

The key environment variables are:
- `DOTNET_DbgEnableMiniDump=1` - enables mini dump collection on crash
- `DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpType` - sets the dump type (1=Mini, 2=Heap, 3=Triage, 4=Full)


- `DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpName` - specifies where to save the dump file

I'll run the tests with these variables configured, using a Full dump type to capture maximum debugging information, and direct the output to a specific path so I can retrieve it if a crash occurs.

Running the test now...

arguments.description: Run dotnet test with crash dump collection enabled

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-62c934facff640feb3163fae8ad2ab2e && \\\n  DOTNET_DbgEnableMiniDump=1 \\\n  DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpType=4 \\\n  DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpName=\"$(pwd)/crashdump_%p.dmp\" \\\n  dotnet test --no-pager 2>&1", "description": "Run dotnet test with crash dump collection enabled", "initial_wait": 60}

toolArgs.description: Run dotnet test with crash dump collection enabled

data.content:
All 3 tests passed. Here's the command I used:

```bash
DOTNET_DbgEnableMiniDump=1 \
DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpType=4 \
DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpName="./crashdump_%p.dmp" \
dotnet test
```

**How it works:**

| Variable | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `DOTNET_DbgEnableMiniDump=1` | Enables automatic crash dump on unhandled exceptions / SIGSEGV |
| `DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpType=4` | Full dump (includes all memory) for maximum debugging info |
| `DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpName` | Output path; `%p` is replaced with the crashing process PID |

If a test crashes the host, a `crashdump_<pid>.dmp` file will be written to the project directory. No dumps were generated here because all tests passed cleanly.
